tech_stack: NoPilot existing architecture (command-driven + file artifacts + Agent execution)
exclusions: No new runtime or external service dependencies

selected_direction.description: Profile-as-Memory with intent/structure separation — a persistent multi-layered project profile that auto-captures architectural intent from NoPilot's own workflow outputs, with intent capture as an experimental layer
selected_direction.differentiator: Only system that auto-captures architectural intent (why, not just what) from structured workflow outputs, without requiring manual knowledge base curation
selected_direction.rationale: NoPilot's discover/spec/build workflow naturally produces structured intent (design philosophy, constraints, invariants, decision rationale). The profile system simply 'catches' these outputs instead of discarding them. No other tool has this structural advantage because no other tool has a structured workflow that produces intent as first-class artifacts.

pre_mortem.scenario: Profile quality degrades, misleading subsequent features
pre_mortem.cause: Agent summarization quality insufficient — writes inaccurate or overly generic profile entries
pre_mortem.early_warning: First feature discover produces requirements that obviously conflict with actual codebase

pre_mortem.cause: User modifies code manually without re-running build, profile drifts from reality
pre_mortem.early_warning: Feature discover proposes approaches that contradict current code structure

pre_mortem.scenario: Mode branching makes discover.md too complex for reliable Agent execution
pre_mortem.cause: 30K prompt + conditional branches per stage causes Agent to skip or misexecute branches
pre_mortem.early_warning: Feature mode stages are inconsistently executed across runs

pre_mortem.scenario: Conflict detection false positives erode user trust
Profile writer misses critical decisions during extraction
pre_mortem.cause: Important decisions are implicit in artifact structure rather than explicitly stated
pre_mortem.early_warning: Profile lacks constraints that users consider obvious

design_philosophy.justification: Refused to maintain separate greenfield/brownfield flows; chose mode triggers within a single flow to reduce maintenance cost and keep concepts unified
Profile content is not newly generated — it catches existing NoPilot workflow outputs (design philosophy, constraints, domain models) that would otherwise be discarded after each run
Stable code structure info and experimental architecture intent are physically isolated, independently toggleable, independently verifiable, independently removable
Each compensation component encodes an assumption about what the model cannot do; when model capability improves, the component should be decisively removed
Avoid projecting local information as global truth. First-time onboarding must not treat a feature's design philosophy as the entire project's philosophy

stack: Storage: .nopilot/profile/ plain JSON files, one per layer
Commands: discover.md, spec.md, build.md (mode branch additions)
Scanning: Agent-driven file analysis, sub-agent parallelization for large codebases
tech_direction.architecture_style: Same as existing NoPilot: command-driven + file artifacts + Agent execution. No new runtimes or services.
tech_direction.product_impact: Zero new dependencies. Profile files are local-only, not git-tracked. All changes are prompt-level (command .md files) + schema additions. Existing greenfield flow unchanged except profile write after build.
tech_direction.rationale: Maintaining architectural consistency with existing NoPilot. The profile system is infrastructure-level but implemented entirely within the existing command/artifact paradigm.

entities.name: ProjectProfile
entities.description: Multi-layered project memory container stored at .nopilot/profile/

entities.name: InfraLayer (L0)
entities.description: Tech stack, language, framework, package manager, CI config

entities.name: ArchLayer (L1)
entities.description: Directory structure, module boundaries, dependency directions, communication patterns, design patterns

entities.name: DecisionLayer (L2)
entities.description: Design philosophy, architecture decision rationale, constraint sources (experimental)

entities.name: StatusLayer (L3)
entities.description: Test coverage, tech debt, change hotspots, domain model snapshot

entities.name: FlowMode
entities.description: greenfield/feature mode identifier, drives conditional behavior at each stage

entities.name: FeatureScope
entities.description: specs/features/feat-xxx/ — isolated artifact space for a feature

entities.name: ConflictReport
entities.description: Comparison result between feature requirements and profile content, with decision options

entities.name: ProfileWriter
entities.description: Logic that extracts info from stage artifacts and writes to profile layers after build

entities.name: ProfileReader
entities.description: Logic that reads profile layers and injects context into stages on demand

relationships.description: Profile existence + codebase existence + user confirmation
Uses L2 as primary anchor for intent conflicts

non_functional_requirements.description: Greenfield mode behavior remains identical to current, with sole addition of profile writing after build
Profile is pure local files, no external service dependencies
L2 decisions layer independently disableable via feature flag
non_functional_requirements.target: Feature flag toggle without side effects

non_functional_requirements.description: Profile read operation must not significantly increase stage startup time
Profile schemas defined as JSON Schema, consistent with existing schema system
Profile read/write interface decoupled from trigger timing for v4 dreaming feature
non_functional_requirements.target: Trigger strategy swappable without changing storage format

non_functional_requirements.description: Profile stored in workspace at .nopilot/, not tracked by git

mvp_features.name: Profile Storage Layer (F1)
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No competitor has multi-layered + intent/structure separation

mvp_features.name: Profile Writer (F2)
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No competitor auto-captures from structured workflow outputs

mvp_features.name: Profile Reader (F3)
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Copilot Workspace generates similar info cold each time

mvp_features.name: Intent Detection + Mode Injection (F4)
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No competitor has mode branching mechanism

mvp_features.name: Codebase Structure Scanning (F6)
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Cursor/Augment do embedding indexing; this is structure-level only

mvp_features.name: Domain Model Incremental Merge (F8)
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No competitor tracks domain model evolution

mvp_features.name: Regression Guard Auto-generation (F9)
mvp_features.competitive_notes: No competitor auto-generates at requirements level

mvp_features.name: Intent Extraction + L2 Write (F13, experimental)
mvp_features.competitive_notes: Core differentiator: only system auto-capturing intent
